params.text:
Do's and don'ts

A few simple habits keep your records safe and reliable.

  • Do write as soon as possible after care, while it is fresh.
  • Do sign, date and time every entry.
  • Do write in permanent black ink on paper records.
  • Do record refusals, concerns and things that went wrong, not just what went well.
  • Don't leave blank lines or gaps that someone could write in later.
  • Don't write in advance or copy the same entry every day without checking.
  • Don't use correction fluid, erase, or scribble out a mistake.
  • Don't guess, exaggerate, or record opinions as facts.

params.question: It is acceptable to write up your daily notes in advance at the start of a shift to save time later.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Correct — this is not acceptable. Records must be contemporaneous, written after care is given. Writing them in advance is dishonest and unsafe.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: That's right to reject it: entries must reflect what actually happened, written at or soon after the time.
